# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Write a Jenkins Pipeline that accepts build parameters from users and executes them as shell commands. Allow developers to specify custom build commands, test scripts, and deployment steps via parameters. This provides maximum flexibility for different project types.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent any
    environment {
        // Set environment variables here if needed
    }
    parameters {
        string(name: 'buildCommand', defaultValue: '', description: 'Custom build command')
        string(name: 'testScript', defaultValue: '', description: 'Custom test script')
        string(name: 'deploymentSteps', defaultValue: '', description: 'Custom deployment steps')
    }
    stages {
        stage('Build') {
            steps {
                // Execute custom build command
                sh "echo Running custom build command: $buildCommand"
                sh "$buildCommand"
            }
        }
        stage('Test') {
            when {
                // Add conditions for running the test stage based on your project requirements
                // For example: branch 'master' && env.TEST
            }
            steps {
                // Execute custom test script
                sh "echo Running custom test script: $testScript"
                sh "$testScript"
            }
        }
        stage('Deploy') {
            when {
                // Add conditions for running the deploy stage based on your project requirements
                // For example: branch 'master' && env.PRODUCTION
            }
            steps {
                // Execute custom deployment steps
                sh "echo Running custom deployment steps: $deploymentSteps"
                sh "$deploymentSteps"
            }
        }
    }
}
